one_line: Gupta emperor who conquered the Western Kshatrapas and presided over the empire’s zenith.

appearance.age_look: Early 30s, athletic medium build, about 5'9" with a poised, balanced stance.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones, straight nose, warm medium-brown complexion, sharp dark eyes with slightly thick brows, a neatly trimmed short moustache and light stubble on the chin, default expression calm and composed with a subtle confident half-smile.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair parted slightly off-center, drawn back and tied into a low, neat topknot at the back crown; a narrow gold circlet with a small central lotus-shaped ornament sits low on the forehead, holding the hair in place.
appearance.costume: Sleeveless, knee-length silk tunic (uttariya-style kurta) in deep maroon with a subtle woven floral pattern and a narrow gold border at the hem and armholes; over the left shoulder a soft, draped saffron-orange silk sash (uttariya) edged with thin green and gold stripes, wrapped once around the torso and hanging behind; lower body clad in finely pleated off-white cotton dhoti wrapped tightly around the waist and between the legs in Gupta style, with a maroon and gold border at the edge; around his waist a broad, flat gold belt with a central rectangular clasp engraved with a simple lotus motif, from which hangs a short maroon fabric panel at the front; he wears simple gold armlets on both upper arms, a pair of slim gold bangles on each wrist, a single short gold necklace with a round pendant, and small round gold stud earrings; on his feet, brown leather sandals with a toe loop and simple straps, appropriate for court and campaign.
appearance.signature: A Gupta-style silver coin held between his right thumb and forefinger, showing a stylized standing king with a long sceptre on one face and a seated goddess on the reverse, which he sometimes flips or displays as a symbol of his conquest of the Western Kshatrapas.

ref_desc: Chandragupta II Vikramaditya is a medium-built, early-30s Gupta emperor with an oval medium-brown face, short moustache, black hair in a low topknot under a narrow gold circlet, wearing a deep maroon sleeveless silk tunic, saffron sash, off-white bordered dhoti, gold ornaments, brown sandals, and holding a Gupta-style silver coin.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character model of Chandragupta II Vikramaditya: a medium-built man in his early 30s with an oval medium-brown face, sharp dark eyes, high cheekbones, a neat short moustache and light chin stubble, black hair pulled back into a low topknot held by a narrow gold circlet with a small central lotus ornament. He wears a sleeveless, knee-length deep maroon silk tunic with a subtle floral weave and a narrow gold border at the hem and armholes, and over his left shoulder a soft saffron-orange silk sash edged with thin green and gold stripes, wrapped once across his torso and trailing behind. His lower body is clothed in a finely pleated off-white cotton dhoti in Gupta style, wrapped snugly with a maroon-and-gold border visible at the edge, secured by a broad flat gold waist-belt with a central rectangular lotus-engraved clasp and a short maroon fabric panel hanging in front. Add gold armlets on both upper arms, two slim gold bangles on each wrist, a short gold necklace with a round pendant, and small round gold stud earrings, and put brown leather sandals with a toe loop on his feet. In his right hand he delicately holds a Gupta-style silver coin between thumb and forefinger, its design hinted as a standing king with sceptre on one side and a seated goddess on the other; arrange a neutral standing pose and a few turns to clearly show his face, hairstyle, circlet, costume details, jewellery, sandals, and the silver coin, using deep maroon, saffron orange, off-white, gold, and brown as dominant colours.
